message:
feat(embed): add streaming model downloads, manifest validation, and batch size clamping

Overhaul the model download pipeline to stream directly to disk instead
of buffering in memory, add comprehensive manifest validation, and
expand test coverage across the embedding subsystem.

model_download.rs:
- Replace in-memory download buffering with streaming to temp file
  using poll_fn for efficient frame-based streaming
- Add unique staging directory creation with collision retry (up to 64
  attempts) for safe concurrent downloads
- Add TempFileGuard struct for automatic cleanup on failed downloads
- Add helper functions: create_unique_staging_dir(), response_content_length(),
  sha256_digest_hex(), atomic_replace_file()
- Validate manifest is production-ready before initiating download
- Enhance progress reporting to update per-frame instead of at completion
- Replace blocking sleep with async asupersync::time::sleep()
- Add HTTP test server infrastructure with 5 tests covering: successful
  download, retry after transient errors, max retry exhaustion, hash
  mismatch detection, and manifest validation enforcement

model_manifest.rs:
- Add 20+ new tests covering: JSON round-trip with field validation,
  missing required field error messages, hash/size format validation,
  manifest catalog validation, model state and consent serialization,
  lifecycle state machine operations, file promotion and backup handling,
  SHA-256 hash validation, download consent resolution with environment
  variable overrides, and manifest property detection methods

batch_coalescer.rs:
- Clamp max_batch_size from 0 to 1 with warning log to prevent
  division-by-zero in batch scheduling
- Fix float equality comparisons to use epsilon-based checks
- Add zero_max_batch_size_is_clamped_to_one test

model_registry.rs:
- Add tests for model candidate path ordering and layout detection
  (legacy model.onnx and HuggingFace snapshot layouts)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
